revision_summary:
Three targeted fixes required before Stage 3 is production-ready:

1. SCHEMA MISUSE — cherry_pick_moments[3] (ts 01:06): Remove the cross_sell_signals.roof_replacement_planned reference. Malcolm's tree-removal comment is not a roof cross-sell signal. Reclassify the moment using analysis_directives.customer_state_trajectory or coaching_philosophy.curiosity_over_features. Drop the invented 'enthusiasm_signal' classification label and use a schema-grounded label.

2. INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY — rep_intent vs cherry_pick_moments[3]: Stage 3 labels Bucky's 'get an estimate' pivot as reactive/accidental in rep_intent but then correctly identifies it as 'the right instinct' and 'letting the design do the work' in cherry_pick_moments[3]. Reconcile: either the pivot is reactive (and cherry_pick_moments[3] needs revision) or it's a deliberate curiosity-creation move (and rep_intent needs revision). The evidence better supports the strategic read — see flipped_hypotheses. The alternative_hypothesis at 00:27 (Bucky deliberately skipped empathy to avoid re-anchoring) should also be resolved rather than raised and dropped.

3. MISSED MOMENTS — add two cherry_pick entries: (a) ts 02:04 area — Bucky skips the 'interest in solar' verify item entirely (schema verify.interest_in_solar, labeled 'THE EMOTIONAL ANCHOR'); this is a distinct coaching gap from the bill flip miss and should be its own moment. (b) ts 02:25 — credit qualifier execution: Bucky states the correct 670 threshold (positive note) but Malcolm's 'Mhmm' confirmation is ambiguous; worth flagging per the schema's unresolved policy question on stated-credit DQ.

Secondary (non-blocking) fixes: (a) Soften trajectory_verdict.summary — remove 'energy leaked through the bill-photo process' since no customer-side evidence supports Malcolm's engagement declining during the wait; reframe as rep-side efficiency gap only. (b) Note the malformed title/co-owner question at 01:33 ('anyone else in the town') in the handoff_brief.unresolved_concerns — if Malcolm has a spouse on title who wasn't properly captured, the appointment is structurally vulnerable per protocols.spouse_decision.
